cards.back: A technique used in distributed systems to enable a program to request services from a remote machine
A model of interaction between processes in a distributed system, where a process sends a request to another process and receives a reply
A request-to-response model where a program invokes a procedure on a remote machine, and the result is sent back to the invoker
A programming technique that allows an object to invoke methods on another object in a different address space, as if the objects were in the same address space
A mechanism that enables a program to invoke methods on an object remotely, allowing for loose coupling and scalability in distributed systems
